title: Cricothyroid Pressure
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Where direct visualisation of the vocal cords is not possible during initial laryngoscopy.
- To compress the oesophagus, preventing gastric contents from entering the airway during intubation.

#### Procedure

- Locate the cricoid cartilage (below the “Adam’s Apple”) (see **Figure 1**).

- Using the thumb and index fingers of one hand, firmly push the cricoid cartilage backwards.

- Maintain pressure until the intubating paramedic directs a release of the pressure post ET tube insertion.

Figure 1
